title: campanula humilis
intro: Campanula humilis, also known as dwarf bellflower, is a low-growing, flowering plant native to Europe and Western Asia. Its roots and leaves have been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Eastern European folk medicine, where they are valued for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and acting as a diuretic. Campanula humilis can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall health and well-being.
